Description: Tall swaying grass under an open sky — the first steps into the wild.
A shallow den dug into a grassy hillside, animal tracks leading inward.
A shallow, clear stream cutting a lazy path through the grass.
Gentle green hills rolling toward the horizon, wind bending the grass in waves.
A low ridge overlooking the meadow, the wind carrying scent for miles.
Coarse thistles and wildflowers crowd this uneven stretch of ground.
A still pond where herds once drank before the tall grass grew wild.
A den carved beneath a massive fallen tree at the meadow's edge.
Bright wildflowers stretch as far as the eye can see, buzzing quietly with life.
A deep den at the meadow's edge, marked with the claw-scored territory of something that rules here.

Visual_Theme: Bright turquoise shallows over a pale sandbar
Description: Sunlit water barely waist-deep, calm until something pale slides beneath the surface.
A stretch of wet sand and grounded driftwood, stranded sea life still twitching in the shallow pools.

Visual_Theme: Small tidewashed grotto at the cliff base
Description: A shallow sea cave just above the waterline, damp and narrow, the first sign of deeper caverns to come.

Visual_Theme: Dense kelp forest swaying in the current
Description: Visibility drops to almost nothing between the kelp fronds; things brush past unseen.

Visual_Theme: Half-submerged ruins guarded by a barbed shape
Description: An old shrine swallowed by the tide, its altar circled endlessly by a scarred ray.

Visual_Theme: Tidepool flats cut by narrow channels
Description: Dozens of shallow pools connected by trickling channels, each one hiding something.

Visual_Theme: Undertow channel with a fast-pulling current
Description: The current here is strong enough to drag careless feet — and stronger things use it to hunt.
Jagged reef spires break the waterline, encrusted with barnacles and old wreckage.

Visual_Theme: Deep current sinkhole descending into dark water
Description: The cave floor drops away into black water with no visible bottom.

Visual_Theme: Flooded trench entrance, key-locked stone door
Description: The zone's final passage — a drowned stone archway that only opens for a bearer of the trench key.
Cracked earth stretching to the horizon, heat shimmering above sand-scoured stone.
Dunes littered with bleached bones, the wind carving new shapes hourly.
A collapsed tunnel network beneath the dunes, walls smoothed by something massive passing through.
A dry lakebed of blinding white salt, mirages bending the air.
A lone stone spire rising from the sand, wrapped in the coiled shed-skin of something enormous.
A rare pocket of green around a spring-fed pool, the only water for miles.
Towering rock formations sculpted smooth by centuries of sandstorm.
A shallow cave baked hot even in shadow, its floor littered with molted scales.
A narrow pass between dunes where tracks converge from every direction.
A deep burrow chamber lined with the fused bones of a dozen swallowed prey.
Thick silk strands stretch between every trunk, glistening with dew and worse.
A tunnel lined with wax-like secretion, the buzzing audible before the entrance appears.
A clearing carpeted in shed exoskeletons, crunching underfoot.
A humid cavern packed with pale, pulsing egg-sacs stacked to the ceiling.
A massive silk-wrapped structure at the thicket's heart, guarded without pause.
Tall grass alive with constant motion, the air thick with wings.
A still, algae-choked pond at the thicket's border, larvae writhing just beneath the surface.
A narrow tunnel nearly sealed by layers of old webbing.
A wide clearing littered with discarded shells of every size.
A cavern swollen with silk and eggs, the true source of the thicket's swarm.
A narrow crack in the cliffside, just wide enough to squeeze through sideways.
A precarious ledge along the cliff face, loose scree shifting underfoot.
A tight crevice crossed with thick strands of webbing anchored to bare rock.
A small cave floor covered in discarded carapace fragments.
A deep vertical crack where something ancient has been shedding skins for decades.
A steep, unstable slope of loose stone, every step risking a slide.
A thin waterfall feeding a small pool wedged between rock walls.
A low cavern floor littered with the picked-clean remains of past prey.
A wide cavern with claw-carved alcoves along every wall.
A cathedral-sized cavern dominated by a mound of fused shells and old kills.
A narrow trail clinging to the mountainside, gusts strong enough to stagger.
Sheer rock face pocked with nests, wings circling constantly overhead.
A cave carved by centuries of lightning strikes, scorch marks on every wall.
A jagged ridgeline littered with old kills and dropped feathers.
A massive nest built atop the highest visible peak, lightning crackling above it.
A wind-scoured peak where snow never quite melts, visibility dropping to nothing.
A still, glacier-fed lake tucked into a hidden basin below the peaks.
A resonant cave where every sound returns tenfold, unsettling in the dark.
A narrow natural rock bridge spanning a deep chasm, wind howling below.

Boss_Name: The Roost of the First Storm
Visual_Theme: The Roost of the First Storm
Description: A wind-torn summit shrine where the oldest and largest of the peaks' hunters nests.
Murky water thick with reeds, ripples betraying movement just below.
A wide expanse of sucking mud, footing uncertain with every step.
A half-flooded cave carved into the bog's edge, water dripping steadily from above.
A dense marsh alive with constant croaking, impossible to pinpoint its source.
An overgrown ruin half-submerged in bog water, guarded by something ancient and patient.
A cave formed beneath massive bog-tree roots, damp and close.
A cluster of weeping willows draped low over stagnant, still water.
A submerged walkway barely visible beneath murky, algae-thick water.
A low, water-filled tunnel that floods further with every step deeper.
A wide, black pool at the bog's deepest point, undisturbed for longer than anyone can say.
A cave entrance high on a sea cliff, the sound of dripping water echoing from within.
A tunnel lined with faintly luminous fungus, the only light source this deep.
A partially submerged chamber, cold water rising to the waist.
A cavern webbed floor to ceiling in pale fungal threads, pulsing faintly.
A vast, glowing flooded cavern where something vast drifts just beneath the light.
A still pool so dark it seems to swallow the light entirely.
A vertical shaft coated in fungal growth, spores drifting like slow snow.
A fully flooded tunnel requiring a held breath and steady nerves.
A cave whose walls seem to shift and pulse, some of it clearly alive.
A massive fungal-mollusk hybrid mass has claimed this cavern as its own, unfurling as you enter.
Veins of raw gemstone catch the light at the cave's entrance, unnervingly beautiful.
Crystal formations scatter torchlight into a hundred shifting, ghostly shapes.
A wide crystal chamber where sound seems to vanish the moment it's made.
An underground pool so clear and still it mirrors the cave ceiling perfectly.
An ornate crystal sarcophagus chamber, faint sobbing echoing from no visible source.
Crystal veins here run a deep, unsettling red, casting the tunnel in crimson light.
A flooded burial chamber, old grave goods glinting beneath the water.
A narrow tunnel where the crystal formations seem to murmur just below hearing.
A grand chamber that was once clearly ceremonial, now cold and abandoned.
The deepest tomb in the cave, sealed for a reason nobody living remembers.
Cracked black glass stretches for miles, heat rippling up from below.
A tunnel lined with old furnace scaffolding, the metal still warm to the touch.
A cavern floor thick with cooled slag and half-melted machinery.
A pool of superheated mineral water, bubbling steadily at the surface.
The central forge chamber, where the zone's constructs are said to be made.
A ridge of black rock under a constant drift of falling ash.
A wide cavern packed with the inert husks of failed constructs.
An underground reservoir kept at a rolling boil by unknown means.
A narrow tunnel lit only by veins of glowing ember embedded in the rock.
The tower's deepest known chamber — where its ultimate construct is still being built.
